issues_classified.description: H_Mech self-contradiction RECURRING from iteration 3: Paper claims 'B≈D confirms encoder-driven' but B=0.076 is 4.5x larger than D=0.017. The correct interpretation is that encoder alignment creates absorption (B>>A), but decoder training SUPPRESSES it in joint training (D<B). The decoder's regulatory role is the central finding, not a failed equivalence test.
issues_classified.suggestion: Reword to: 'Encoder alignment is necessary and sufficient to create absorption (B>>A), but decoder training during joint training plays a regulatory/suppressive role that reduces absorption below encoder-only levels (D<B).' Update abstract, Section 4.1, and introduction to explicitly frame D<B as the key decoder-suppression finding. Estimated 30 min.

issues_classified.description: H_Safe placeholder features RECURRING from iteration 3: Indices (1024, 2048, 3072, 4096, 5120) are NOT validated Neuronpedia safety features. Paper claims 'null absorption for safety-critical features' but this measures arbitrary SAE indices. Section 5.3 Safety Implications is built on no actual evidence.
issues_classified.suggestion: Remove all claims about safety features based on placeholder data. Restructure H_Safe as 'Methodology Proposed, Pending Validated Neuronpedia Annotations.' Explicitly state that placeholder indices prevent any conclusion. Do not present as a null result. Estimated 15 min.

issues_classified.description: Sensitivity metric values >1 (L0=16: 1.525) RECURRING from iteration 3: Sensitivity defined as Var(Δlogits | Δa_f) should be bounded [0,1]. Values >1 suggest formula bug or missing normalization. Pareto frontier shape (R²=0.963 in pilot) depends on these values.
issues_classified.suggestion: Verify sensitivity metric formula implementation. Rescale to [0,1] or document the scale. If raw variance is used without normalization, fix and re-run H_Pareto analysis. Full experiment found degenerate results (absorption=0 across all L0), making this less urgent but the formula should still be verified for methodological integrity. Estimated 1 hour.

issues_classified.description: Abstract-body pilot vs full number inconsistency RECURRING from iteration 3: Abstract reports 'B=0.076 vs D=0.017' (seed-42 pilot) but body reports full 5-seed results (B=0.055, D=0.017, delta=0.037). Writing review flags this as CRITICAL.
issues_classified.suggestion: Update abstract to report full 5-seed results (B=0.055, D=0.017, delta=0.037) or clearly label the abstract numbers as 'pilot experiment (seed 42).' Estimated 10 min.

issues_classified.description: Figure 2 referenced in wrong place NEW in iteration 4: Section 2.2 says 'Figure 2 illustrates the absorption phenomenon conceptually' but Figure 2 is the H_Mech factorial bar chart. Conceptual illustration is Figure 1. Writing review flags as CRITICAL.
issues_classified.suggestion: Change 'Figure 2' to 'Figure 1' in Section 2.2 reference. Add Figure 2 reference to Section 4.1 where factorial results are discussed. Estimated 10 min.

issues_classified.description: MCC ~0.21 chance-level recovery RECURRING from iteration 3: Hungarian matching on overcomplete dictionaries yields chance-level recovery regardless of training. If SAEs aren't recovering ground-truth features, 'absorption reduction' may reflect sparsity-induced suppression rather than genuine hierarchical feature recovery.
issues_classified.suggestion: Add explicit discussion in Section 5.5 Limitations: If MCC is chance-level, absorption reduction may not reflect genuine hierarchical feature recovery. This fundamentally affects interpretation of all absorption measurements. Estimated 15 min.

issues_classified.description: H_Comp statistical fragility RECURRING from iteration 3: r~+0.93 sparsity-absorption correlation based on n=4 with two points at identical L0=50. Driven by only 3 unique L0 values. Bootstrap CI doesn't fix fundamental sample-size limitation.
issues_classified.suggestion: Acknowledge n=4 as insufficient for reliable correlation. Either replicate at additional unique L0 values or reframe as preliminary evidence requiring validation. Estimated 15 min.

issues_classified.description: Section 3.5 promises ANOVA not delivered RECURRING from iteration 3: Paper promises one-way ANOVA across all completed variants but this is not reported. Pairwise effect sizes are more appropriate given small N but unfulfilled promise undermines credibility.
issues_classified.suggestion: Remove ANOVA promise from Section 3.5, or actually conduct and report the ANOVA. Alternatively, reframe Section 3.5 as describing what comparisons WILL be made, not what test WILL be run. Estimated 10 min.

issues_classified.description: Pilot R²=0.963 still referenced in abstract NEW in iteration 4: The pilot R² appears in abstract and Section 5.2 but full experiments found degenerate results (absorption=0 across all L0 levels). Pilot R² is no longer relevant.
issues_classified.suggestion: Remove pilot R²=0.963 from abstract and Section 5.2. State 'full experiments found degenerate results (absorption=0 across all L0 levels), indicating no trade-off frontier exists in synthetic SAEs' without referencing pilot R². Estimated 10 min.

issues_classified.description: Figure 1 not previewed in Introduction NEW in iteration 4: Outline promises 'Figure reference: Figure 1 (teaser - key result)' in Introduction contributions roadmap but paper never previews Figure 1 there.
issues_classified.suggestion: Add 'This key result is illustrated in Figure 1' to the Contributions roadmap paragraph in the Introduction. Estimated 5 min.

issues_classified.description: Section 5.2 circular sentence RECURRING from iteration 3: 'Our empirical results reveal that sensitivity and absorption trade off approximately as sensitivity decreases as absorption increases' is tautological.
issues_classified.suggestion: Delete the tautological sentence. The paragraph following explains the actual findings (degenerate result) clearly without it. Estimated 5 min.

issues_classified.description: Vague language: 'fundamentally' and 'overturns' NEW in iteration 4: Section 5.2 'likely fundamental' should specify why. Section 5.1 'overturns the prevailing decoder geometry hypothesis' is too strong given C≈A fails but B≈D holds.
issues_classified.suggestion: Change 'overturns' to 'refines' or 'complicates.' Change 'fundamental' to specific language about why absorption is inherent to the SAE objective. Estimated 5 min.

issues_classified.description: Multi-seed validation now complete: h_mech_full completed (5 seeds), h_pareto_full completed (4 L0 x 3 seeds), held_out_validation completed (3 seeds). This fixes the 'incomplete multi-seed validation' issue from iteration 3.
issues_classified.suggestion: No action needed — this is a success. The paper should clearly report multi-seed statistics in Tables 2-4 and acknowledge the full experiment results.

issues_classified.description: All 5 figures now generated and audit-confirmed: Figures 1-5 exist in PNG and PDF formats. This fixes the 'missing figures' critical issue from iteration 3.
issues_classified.suggestion: No action needed — this is a success. Remaining figure issue is purely referential (wrong figure referenced in Section 2.2).

H_Safe correctly labeled as INCONCLUSIVE in Section 4.4 with caveat 'placeholder feature selection prevents firm conclusions' — partially improved from iteration 3

E2 meta-analysis uses N=314 with partial correlation and cluster-robust SEs — strongest empirical signal in the paper

Full multi-seed validation achieved: 5-seed H_Mech, 3-seed H_Comp, 3-seed H_Pareto, 3-seed held-out validation
Claim-evidence alignment maintained: Quantitative claims match source data exactly across all sections

H_Safe placeholder features persisted from iteration 1 to iteration 4 — never properly validated or removed from paper claims

suggestions: GPU resources fully utilized — no idle time

Writing revision is the bottleneck: 4 consecutive CRITICAL issues (H_Mech interpretation, H_Safe placeholders, abstract-body inconsistency, Figure 2 reference) persist from supervisor/critic review to next iteration without being fixed

Consider parallelizing writing revision tasks: multiple independent text changes (Figure 2 reference, abstract numbers, Section 5.2 tautology, pilot R² removal) can be done concurrently in a single revision pass
Add pre-writing verification step: before entering writing_revision, verify all CRITICAL/MEDIUM issues from supervisor/critic are addressed
